 {"id":"bd-lovr","title":"Reject multiple RAID profile bits in sys_chunk_array","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T12:18:21.472435981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T12:28:19.619657772Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T12:28:19.619379442Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-lqewd","title":"Reject partial btrfs root item extension fields","description":"parse_root_item accepts impossible partial extension tails: bytes after the 239-byte legacy layout that do not contain a complete generation_v2 field, and partial UUID/parent_uuid fragments when generation_v2 marks UUID-era fields valid. Reject partial extension fragments while preserving exact legacy payloads and full supported extension fields; add focused parser/golden coverage and parity docs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-27T18:46:40.438685521Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T18:55:27.131175438Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T18:55:27.129308867Z","close_reason":"Rejected partial btrfs ROOT_ITEM extension fields and added parser/golden coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","conformance"]}
 {"id":"bd-lqwc","title":"Add E2E test suite for graceful degradation under load","description":"# Add E2E test suite for graceful degradation under load

## GOAL
Validate the graceful degradation system works correctly under real system pressure.

## BACKGROUND
The Graceful Degradation system (bd-3tz) must:
- Detect system pressure correctly
- Transition between degradation levels
- Recover when pressure relieved
- Never cause data loss

## TEST SCRIPT: scripts/e2e/ffs_degradation_stress.sh

### 1. CPU Pressure Tests
```bash
test_cpu_pressure_degradation() {
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --rw &
    FFS_PID=$!
    
    # Start with baseline measurements
    baseline_latency=$(measure_read_latency "$MNT")
    
    # Apply CPU pressure (16 stress processes)
    stress-ng --cpu 16 --timeout 30s &
    STRESS_PID=$!
    
    sleep 5
    
    # Measure degraded performance
    stressed_latency=$(measure_read_latency "$MNT")
    
    # Reads should still work (degraded but functional)
    assert_reads_work "$MNT"
    
    # Degradation level should have increased
    level=$(get_degradation_level_from_tui)
    assert_gte "$level" 1
    
    # Kill stress
    kill $STRESS_PID
    wait $STRESS_PID 2>/dev/null
    
    sleep 5
    
    # Should recover to normal
    recovered_latency=$(measure_read_latency "$MNT")
    assert_within_factor "$recovered_latency" "$baseline_latency" 2
    
    kill $FFS_PID
}
```

### 2. Memory Pressure Tests
```bash
test_memory_pressure_cache_reduction() {
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --rw --cache-size 256M &
    FFS_PID=$!
    
    # Fill cache
    read_many_files "$MNT"
    initial_cache_size=$(get_cache_size)
    
    # Apply memory pressure
    stress-ng --vm 4 --vm-bytes 80% --timeout 30s &
    STRESS_PID=$!
    
    sleep 5
    
    # Cache should have reduced
    pressured_cache_size=$(get_cache_size)
    assert_lt "$pressured_cache_size" "$initial_cache_size"
    
    # Reads should still work
    assert_reads_work "$MNT"
    
    # OOM killer should NOT be invoked
    assert_no_oom
    
    kill $STRESS_PID
    wait $STRESS_PID 2>/dev/null
    
    kill $FFS_PID
}
```

### 3. Degradation Level Progression
```bash
test_degradation_level_progression() {
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --rw &
    FFS_PID=$!
    
    # Level 0: Normal
    assert_level 0
    assert_scrub_running
    assert_gc_running
    
    # Level 1: Background paused
    apply_light_pressure
    wait_for_level 1
    assert_scrub_paused
    
    # Level 2: Cache reduced
    apply_medium_pressure
    wait_for_level 2
    assert_cache_reduced
    
    # Level 3: Writes throttled
    apply_heavy_pressure
    wait_for_level 3
    assert_writes_throttled
    
    # Level 4: Read-only
    apply_extreme_pressure
    wait_for_level 4
    assert_writes_rejected_erofs
    assert_reads_work
    
    # Recovery
    relieve_all_pressure
    wait_for_level 0
    assert_normal_operation
    
    kill $FFS_PID
}
```

### 4. No Data Loss Under Pressure
```bash
test_no_data_loss_under_pressure() {
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --rw &
    FFS_PID=$!
    
    # Start continuous writes
    continuous_write "$MNT/datafile" &
    WRITE_PID=$!
    
    # Apply varying pressure
    for pressure_level in light medium heavy; do
        apply_pressure "$pressure_level"
        sleep 5
    done
    
    # Stop writes
    kill $WRITE_PID
    wait $WRITE_PID 2>/dev/null
    
    # Sync and unmount
    sync
    kill $FFS_PID
    wait $FFS_PID
    
    # Verify all acknowledged writes persisted
    ffs mount "$IMAGE" "$MNT" --ro
    verify_data_integrity "$MNT/datafile"
}
```

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Log degradation level transitions with timestamp
- Log pressure metrics (CPU, memory, IO)
- Log cache size changes
- Log background task state (scrub, GC)

## FIXTURES
- Use stress-ng for reproducible pressure
- Use cgroups for memory limits if available
- 256MB test image

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Degradation levels transition correctly
2. Recovery works when pressure relieved
3. No data loss at any degradation level
4. No OOM killer invocation
5. Read latency <10x baseline at Level 3","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:13:11.849026407Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T15:44:05.697170888Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T15:44:05.697147454Z","close_reason":"Completed: added scripts/e2e/ffs_degradation_stress.sh, updated scripts/e2e/README.md, and validated via full script run (PASS)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["degradation","e2e","harness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lqwc","depends_on_id":"bd-1bx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:13:21.511279429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lqwc","depends_on_id":"bd-2jk.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:49.307234125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lqwc","depends_on_id":"bd-2r8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:13:21.414406994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lqwc","depends_on_id":"bd-3rix","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:02.733780633Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lqwc","depends_on_id":"bd-3tz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:38.869347159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":90,"issue_id":"bd-lqwc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW: At 175 lines, this is the largest open bead. The 4 test sections (CPU pressure, memory pressure, degradation progression, no-data-loss) are coherent as a single E2E suite. No split needed, but if implementation reveals further complexity, consider splitting CPU/memory pressure into separate beads.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:50Z"},{"id":186,"issue_id":"bd-lqwc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: quality_assurance
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.1 Optimization Loop + 0.7 Artifact Contract + 6.12 Proptest
EV score: 50 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: bench_runs>=10, sample_floor=30 for noisy paths
Fallback trigger: golden-only gate, disable adaptive controller deployment

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-lrh8b","title":"add mounted-path ext4 read-only EROFS coverage for xattr set/remove operations","description":"Survey finding: ffs-fuse already hard-rejects setxattr and removexattr with EROFS on read-only mounts, and ffs-core has read-only mutation coverage, but fuse_e2e does not freeze the operator-visible ext4 contract. Scope: add mounted-path ext4 probes that attempt user.* setxattr and removexattr on a read-only mount and assert exact EROFS plus unchanged file bytes and xattr state. Acceptance: ext4 FUSE E2E proves read-only xattr mutation refusal with no side effects.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:09:38.390987334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","close_reason":"Stale in-progress sweep: no commit reference after more than 12 hours; closed per operator instruction so the work can be re-filed or reclaimed cleanly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 | btrfs internal node parsing | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | `parse_internal_items` in `ffs-ondisk` |
 | btrfs sys_chunk mapping | `fs/btrfs/volumes.c` | ✅ | `map_logical_to_physical` in `ffs-ondisk`, with proptest + metamorphic coverage in `crates/ffs-ondisk/src/btrfs.rs` for translation covariance, chunk-order permutation invariance, and Single-vs-DUP primary mapping equivalence. |
 | btrfs read-only tree walk | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | `walk_tree` in `ffs-btrfs` |
-| btrfs item payload decode (ROOT/INODE/DIR/EXTENT_DATA) | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | Implemented in `ffs-btrfs` (`parse_root_item`, `parse_root_ref`, `parse_inode_item`, `parse_dir_items`, `parse_extent_data`) with direct cargo-fuzz coverage in `fuzz_btrfs_tree_items` for deterministic decode and field-level payload invariants. `parse_root_item()` reads level/UUID fields from the packed kernel layout, invalidates UUID-era fields when `generation_v2` is stale, and rejects impossible tree levels; `parse_root_ref()` rejects ROOT_REF payloads with trailing bytes after the declared name; `parse_dir_items()` rejects nonzero `data_len` payloads that belong to xattr-shaped items instead of directory entries; `parse_inode_item()` rejects payloads that are not the fixed 160-byte `btrfs_inode_item` size and rejects impossible timestamp nanoseconds before they reach `Duration`/`SystemTime` conversion paths; and `parse_extent_data()` rejects unsupported extent compression values and nonzero encryption/other-encoding fields instead of treating encoded payloads as raw readable data. |
+| btrfs item payload decode (ROOT/INODE/DIR/EXTENT_DATA) | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | Implemented in `ffs-btrfs` (`parse_root_item`, `parse_root_ref`, `parse_inode_item`, `parse_dir_items`, `parse_extent_data`) with direct cargo-fuzz coverage in `fuzz_btrfs_tree_items` for deterministic decode and field-level payload invariants. `parse_root_item()` reads level/UUID fields from the packed kernel layout, invalidates UUID-era fields when `generation_v2` is stale, rejects impossible tree levels, and rejects partial extension-field fragments; `parse_root_ref()` rejects ROOT_REF payloads with trailing bytes after the declared name; `parse_dir_items()` rejects nonzero `data_len` payloads that belong to xattr-shaped items instead of directory entries; `parse_inode_item()` rejects payloads that are not the fixed 160-byte `btrfs_inode_item` size and rejects impossible timestamp nanoseconds before they reach `Duration`/`SystemTime` conversion paths; and `parse_extent_data()` rejects unsupported extent compression values and nonzero encryption/other-encoding fields instead of treating encoded payloads as raw readable data. |
 | btrfs open/validate pipeline | `fs/btrfs/disk-io.c` | ✅ | `BtrfsContext` in `ffs-core` |
 | btrfs transaction parity | `fs/btrfs/transaction.c` | ✅ | Transaction begin/commit/abort semantics and integration paths are implemented with coverage in unit + E2E suites. |
 | btrfs delayed refs parity | `fs/btrfs/delayed-ref.c` | ✅ | `DelayedRefQueue` + `BtrfsRef` model + bounded flush/refcount tracking in `ffs-btrfs::BtrfsExtentAllocator`, with queue/refcount/stress tests. The allocator now also exposes fragmentation-aware largest-free-extent discovery from block-group/extent-tree gaps, and `OpenFs::largest_contiguous_free_run` uses that value for btrfs instead of reporting total free bytes as a conservative upper bound. |

 ## Priority Gaps
 
@@ -68,7 +68,7 @@ None. All identified on-disk structures have conformance fixtures.

 | Btrfs dev items | ✅ | ✅ | synthetic adversarial seeds cover full field-layout parsing, max numeric/classification values, trailing bytes after the fixed 98-byte item, zero device ID rejection, zero-capacity device rejection, impossible `bytes_used > total_bytes` accounting, and truncated payload rejection |

 | Btrfs send streams | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover command CRC32C validation, missing-END rejection, unknown command fallback, zero-length and unknown attributes, multi-attribute commands, trailing partial command headers, and END payload TLV validation |
 | Btrfs delayed refs | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover zero-limit flush preservation, bounded partial flush accounting, delete-underflow queue preservation, and sequence-ordered drain across extent key order |
 | Btrfs transactions | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial mutation tests cover same-tree root replacement, delayed-ref commit failure nonvisibility, and tree-root address overflow nonvisibility |
